channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power was restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv on Saturday, a day after Russia launched major attacks on the Ukrainian power grid that caused blackouts across much of the country, and European leaders agreed to proceed toward using hundreds of billions of frozen Russian assets to support Ukrai
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Lee Keath, Julia Frankel And Jalal Bwaital, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Ukraine's capital as child is killed in separate attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drone and missile strikes wounded at least 20 people in Kyiv, damaged residential buildings and caused blackouts across swaths of Ukraine early Friday, authorities said. A child also was killed in separate attacks in the southeast of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize chair responds to Trump's pressure campaign
item.description: The chair of the Nobel Peace Prize committee responded Friday to President Trump's public pressure campaign to win the award, telling reporters that the deliberation process for choosing the awardee prioritizes courage and integrity.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Macron asks newly resigned Prime Minister Lecornu to try again to form a government
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Friday re-appointed Sebastien Lecornu as prime minister, just days after his resignation, to try again to form a government in a bid to end the country’s political deadlock.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Madagascar's Gen Z protesters clash with police as they call for president to step down
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — About 1,000 protesters marched in Madagascar's capital on Thursday, clashing with police who used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse them in the third week of what has been the most significant unrest in years in the Indian Ocean island nation.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TÉTAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Nobel Peace Prize nominations
item.description: Throughout his second term, President Trump has campaigned for the Nobel Peace Prize. The president, his allies and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have renewed the push since Israel and Hamas agreed to the first phase of a peace plan Trump proposed last month.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: UN is ready to surge aid into Gaza and waiting for green light from Israel after deal
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations said Thursday that 170,000 metric tons of food, medicine and other humanitarian aid is ready to enter Gaza and that it is seeking a green light from Israel to massively increase help for more than 2 million Palestinians following a deal to pause the war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders express hope after Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of plan to end Gaza war
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tropical Storm Jerry churns in Atlantic as weakened Priscilla nears Mexico
item.description: Priscilla lost its hurricane status and was downgraded to a tropical storm as it churned up Mexico's western Pacific coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's new missiles and drones cause gas shortages in Russia, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Strikes on Russian oil facilities by Ukraine’s newly developed long-range missiles and drones are causing significant gas shortages in Russia, while on the battlefield a recent Ukrainian counter-offensive has derailed Russia’s plans to capture parts of the eastern Donetsk region
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Celebrations in Israel, Gaza at first peace deal announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump says the remaining Israeli hostages in Gaza may be released as soon as Monday.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Manchester synagogue attacker 'pledged allegiance to Islamic State,' police say
item.description: The suspect in the deadly attack on a synagogue in Manchester allegedly pledged allegiance to the Islamic State in a call to emergency dispatchers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greta Thunberg slammed for using image of starved Israeli hostage to show Palestinians suffering in Gaza
item.description: Greta Thunberg is being criticized after using a photo of starving hostage Evyatar David in an Instagram post claiming to show Palestinians suffering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Deadline for Mexico to pay water owed to US fast approaching
item.description: The clock is ticking down on Mexico's deadline this month to pay the United States water it owes under a 1944 international treaty.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza, detains scores of activists
item.description: The ministry said the 145 activists, who were in good health, were being brought to shore in Israel for processing and are expected to be deported soon.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Priscilla nears major hurricane status in Pacific as tropical storm swirls in Atlantic
item.description: Just off the west coast of Mexico, Priscilla was spinning with maximum sustained winds around 105 mph and moving northwest at 9 mph.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Newly elected German mayor fights for life after stabbing attack
item.description: German mayor-elect Iris Stalzer was hospitalized in critical condition following a stabbing attack at her home in the German town of Herdecke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Big Time Rush's James Maslow releases new song celebrating Israel
item.description: Maslow acknowledged he chose Oct. 7th because of how the "country (Israel) and this world have normalized a hate towards each other.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says it struck Russian ammunition plant, oil terminal and weapons depot
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Long-range Ukrainian drones and missiles hit a major Russian ammunition plant, a key oil terminal and an important weapons depot behind the front line, Ukraine's president and military said Monday, as Kyiv cranked up pressure on Moscow’s military logistics.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French Prime Minister resigns after less than a month in office
item.description: Facing criticism from all sides, France’s new prime minister Sébastien Lecornu resigned less than 24 hours after naming his government
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas prepare for negotiations in Egypt ahead of possible Gaza ceasefire
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas prepared for indirect negotiations in Egypt on Monday, as hopes for a possible ceasefire in Gaza grew after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said a hostage release could be announced this week. Tuesday marks two years since the Hamas attack that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK police given more time to question 4 suspects over deadly Manchester synagogue attack
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. police were given extra time Saturday to question four people arrested on suspicion of terror offenses after an attack on a synagogue in northwest England that left two people dead and Britain’s Jewish community shocked and grieving.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
